evidence: Toolkit types.ts has comprehensive typed contracts: ToolResult<T>, DemosError with typed codes, SdkBridge interface with 15+ typed methods
67 any-typed locations across src/ (excluding tests) — concentrated in plugins, lib/pipeline, and SDK boundary code
17 `catch (err: any)` patterns vs 106 `catch (e: unknown)` — mostly migrated but holdouts remain
V2SessionState.engagements is typed as any[] — no typed engagement shape exists
3 plugins inject undeclared fields into V2SessionState via (ctx.state as any).fieldName — oracleSnapshot, priceSnapshot not on the type
2 plugins use (ctx.state as any).signalSnapshot and briefingContext — but these fields ARE on V2SessionState, so the casts are unnecessary
sources-plugin.ts uses (ctx: any) for hook callbacks where typed BeforePublishDraftContext and AfterPublishDraftContext exist
tlsn-playwright-bridge.ts has 12+ as any casts — justified SDK boundary but no DemosExtended-style interface covering all casts
feed-filter.ts any types appear to have been fixed (no any found in current file)

type_safety.dimension_character: The toolkit layer has strong type safety — typed error codes, generic ToolResult<T>, and comprehensive SdkBridge interface. However, the plugin and lib layers have a persistent any-type problem. 67 any-typed locations remain, concentrated in plugins that inject undeclared fields into session state via as-any casts, hook callbacks typed as any despite typed contexts existing, and catch blocks using any instead of unknown. The V2SessionState type itself has an any[] for engagements and is missing fields that plugins write to at runtime. A developer working in the toolkit would trust the types; a developer working in the plugins or lib layer would encounter regular gaps.
type_safety.score_rationale: This scores below the 80 anchor because a developer encounters enough any types in the plugin and lib layers to undermine type trust. The 67 any-typed locations are not cosmetic — they include undeclared state fields (oracleSnapshot, priceSnapshot), hook callbacks with available typed alternatives, and state interfaces using any[]. The toolkit layer's strong typing pulls the score up from the 60 range, but the two-tier quality creates friction when code crosses from toolkit into lib/plugins.

issues.summary: 3 plugins inject oracleSnapshot, priceSnapshot into V2SessionState via (as any) — fields not declared on type
evidence: sc-oracle-plugin.ts line 32: (ctx.state as any).oracleSnapshot = result.data — field not on V2SessionState
sc-prices-plugin.ts line 36: (ctx.state as any).priceSnapshot = result.data — field not on V2SessionState
V2SessionState in state.ts has signalSnapshot and briefingContext but not oracleSnapshot or priceSnapshot
issues.suggestion: Add `oracleSnapshot?: unknown` and `priceSnapshot?: unknown` to V2SessionState in state.ts. Then remove the as-any casts in the plugins.

issues.summary: signals-plugin.ts uses (ctx.state as any) for fields that exist on V2SessionState
evidence: Line 36: (ctx.state as any).signalSnapshot — but signalSnapshot is declared on V2SessionState
Line 40: (ctx.state as any).briefingContext — but briefingContext is declared on V2SessionState
BeforeSenseContext.state is typed as V2SessionState, so these assignments work without casts
issues.suggestion: Remove the as-any casts: `ctx.state.signalSnapshot = signalResult.value` and `ctx.state.briefingContext = briefingResult.value`.

issues.summary: sources-plugin.ts uses (ctx: any) for hook callbacks where typed contexts exist
evidence: Line 90: `beforePublishDraft: async (ctx: any)` — BeforePublishDraftContext exists in extensions.ts
Line 97: `afterPublishDraft: async (ctx: any)` — AfterPublishDraftContext exists in extensions.ts
issues.suggestion: Import BeforePublishDraftContext and AfterPublishDraftContext from extensions.ts and replace the any annotations.

issues.summary: V2SessionState.engagements typed as any[] — no engagement result type exists
evidence: Line 132: `engagements: any[]` — the engagement results pushed to this array have a known shape (reaction decisions with txHash, type, reason)
SessionState (v1) has `engagements: Record<string, unknown>[]` which is marginally better
issues.suggestion: Define an EngagementRecord interface with the known fields (txHash, reactionType, reason, timestamp) and use `EngagementRecord[]` instead of `any[]`.

issues.summary: 17 catch blocks use `catch (err: any)` instead of `catch (e: unknown)` with type narrowing
evidence: 17 instances of `catch (err: any)` or `catch (e: any)` in non-test src/ code
106 instances already use `catch (e: unknown)` or bare `catch {}` — the correct pattern is established
The toErrorMessage() utility from lib/util/errors.ts exists specifically for safe error narrowing
issues.suggestion: Replace `catch (err: any)` with `catch (err: unknown)` and use `toErrorMessage(err)` for the message. This is a straightforward find-and-replace across 17 sites.

issues.summary: engage-heuristics.ts uses any for post types where ScanPost type exists
evidence: Line 28: `remainingPosts: any[]` — ScanPost type from toolkit/types.ts describes this shape
Line 47: `post: any` parameter — same issue
issues.suggestion: Import ScanPost from toolkit/types.ts (no SDK deps) and replace any with ScanPost.

root_causes: Plugin state injection uses as-any because fields were added to plugins before being declared on the state type — missing coordination between plugin and state type evolution
catch (err: any) is a holdover from pre-TypeScript strict migration that was partially but not fully completed
likely_symptoms: signals_plugin_unnecessary_casts is a symptom of the broader plugin as-any pattern — the casts were likely copy-pasted from plugins that genuinely needed them
possible_false_positives: feed-filter.ts appears to have been fixed — the open issue about 10 any annotations may be stale

add.header: Toolkit types are comprehensive and trusted
add.description: ToolResult<T>, DemosError with 10 typed codes, SdkBridge interface with 15+ typed methods, and Zod schemas for all tool inputs provide strong type safety at the toolkit boundary.

add.header: Plugin state injection bypasses type system
add.description: 3 plugins inject fields (oracleSnapshot, priceSnapshot) into V2SessionState via (as any) casts because the fields are not declared on the type. A 4th plugin (signals) uses unnecessary casts for fields that do exist.
106 catch blocks use the correct unknown/bare pattern, but 17 holdouts still use catch (err: any). The toErrorMessage() utility exists for safe narrowing.
Previous review flagged 10 any annotations in feed-filter.ts. Current scan finds zero — the issue appears resolved.
